title: rhus virens var. choriophylla
intro: Rhus virens var. choriophylla, also known as the poison oak, is a specific variety of the poison oak plant, characterized by its unique leaf morphology and toxic properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat various health issues, including skin irritations, wounds, and fever. The medicinal properties of Rhus virens var. choriophylla have been valued for their ability to promote healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make ointments, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
